opp.name: Techtronic Industries S2C
opp.next_step: 50% confidence: 1st July Duncan and Tej advised - likely to now move from S2P to S2C (P2P will come early new year. Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa. Now they want S2C revised pricing and implementation estimates/timelines- They are meeting CFO week of 13th July to decided on scope.This is all coming from the risk they feel moving from ECC to S4Hana- want proper and revised RACI, project plan- like all the other vendors gave them (we didn't) 30th Jun Duncan/Tej are asking for discussions on Implementation options and likely costing to phase- Will update COB 31st slightly positive sign, but is certain we have passed the Reference Check phase 30 Jun - Tej Ram responded to Carl, they have completed references and are going through final stages of process. 26th Jun- follow up to check on status, offer F2F meeting etc 18th June- email update to Sonia and Duncan regarding references- and introducing SEQ (Andre) directly with Phn Number , also included the Seq video from Horizon. 15th Jun- close date moved, as it is unlikely to get awarded and signed in <2 weeks 2th June: provided Nissan as reference, and confirming TataPlay as second reference next week. Then if all good, Testing environment and deep dive/workshop into our Implementation strategy. 6th Jun Accelerated questions, and reference calls being set up (TataPlay, others) Duncan it more technical evaluator- Award date still looks to be in June 25th May: Provided additional information + References. Will review and do reference check, then next step will be sandbox testing, and detailed integration session. 11th May- confirmed by email- shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox- need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here. 28th April: presenting solutions to senior management, then going through a shortlisting processes to 2-3 vendors (within the next 2 weeks) for further evaluations (potential use case demonstrations), then deciding on a vendor by 29th May. 10th April - Updated new timelines again. Decision now 29th May. 27th March: Confirmed new timeline - BAFO 24th April, Vendor Decision 8th May. 18th March: Received additional clarification questions to be completed and sent back by 23rd March. Mentioned the RFP timeframe has changed, will provide us more details shortly. 12th March: Completed onsite demo, they have a few more vendors to review. Will get back in touch once they have provided more indepth use cases to futher demonstrate. 5th March: Onsite presentation/demo for 3 hours scheduled for 10th March. 12th Feb: Submitted online questionnaires (350 questions) and sent Zycus proposal including pricing. Next step is demo/presentation with key stakeholders End of Feb/Early March. Aiming for this to be onsite. 10th Feb: Unable to meet CPO as it was conflict of interest during RFP process. Completion of RFP by the 14th Feb. 22nd Jan - Meeting CPO in Melbourne on 3rd Feb for an in-person meeting to gather further intelligence on their RFP process and where Zycus is currently sitting. 13th Jan Good position- Zycus seen as strong solution. ck to meet CPO next trip - solid Q1 opportunity- vs Coupa/iValua/Ariba 8th Jan: Received all RFP docs except pricing information will be sent on Monday 12th. Expectation to be submitted by 18th Jan. Also a quick briefing on the RFP with their internal stakeholders on Monday 12th Jan. As SC confirmed we are very good fit. 12th Dec - Had quick catch up with Rej, CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January, looking to decide on a vendor by April and start implementation. Will know more once I receive the RFP. 29th Nov - Will be releasing RFP in January to 3-4 vendors for S2P. 11th Nov - Invited to Webinar. Provided RFP template. He is currently working on creating a business case to provide to the board to get approval to start official evaluation. If approved will start evaluating in New Year. 5th Nov- email to set up connect sent. If no further actions required, move back to Initial interest for nuture Sep 11: Tej Ram was re-introduced to Luke Dougherty post Stanley's exit. He attended the EY Melbourne event. July 2: Conducted comprehensive demo to the team. They have requested for a hands on sandbox trial. Currently in process of gathering trial scenarios. June 23: Meeting details on June 18: Please see description below. TTi - Demo on July 2nd. Tej has given the scenarios that were clarified by Tanya. Jue 18, Demo on July 2 has been confirmed. We had an opportunity to clarify what Tej wants to look review. 16th June, 2025 Stanley and Tanya had an onsite catch up meeting with Tej Ram (Manager Procurement & Transformation). Demo scheduled for first week of July. RFP to be expected after a while 20th Jan 2025 Had a demo call with Tej Ram, Sonia Gopalan and Melissa Bartram. Will schedule more demos
opp.ais_why:
• Per the 11 May 2026 rep note: 'confirmed by email- shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox- need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here.' Across 3,282 chars of NextStep spanning Jan 2025 to May 2026 ‚Äî covering RFP, demos, onsite presentations and shortlisting ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, or autonomous despite Merlin, Merlin Intake, and Agentic AI being in product scope.
• On 5 Mar 2026: 'Onsite presentation/demo for 3 hours scheduled for 10th March.' On 12 Mar 2026: 'Completed onsite demo, they have a few more vendors to review.' On 12 Dec 2025: 'CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January, looking to decide on a vendor by April and start implementation.' The decision framework is CFO-led, vendor-comparison-driven (vs GEP/iValua/Coupa/Ariba) with no AI-specific evaluation criterion captured.
• Confidence is Medium: heavy AI product loadout (3 AI SKUs) signals Zycus positioning, but the buyer-side dialogue captured across 16 months is pure traditional S2P RFP ‚Äî onsite demos, BAFO, 'hands on sandbox', local reference. The 4/10 score reflects AI in scope without AI in conversation; would move up only if hands-on sandbox produces AI-specific buyer engagement.

items.quote: They are meeting CFO week of 13th July to decide on scope

items.area: Implementation RACI/project plan not yet delivered
items.quote: want proper and revised RACI, project plan- like all the other vendors gave them (we didn't)

items.area: No transcribed buyer call in the last 30-60 days (recent evidence is email/Next Step only)
items.quote: latest transcribed Avoma call is 12 Jan 2026 / 2 Feb 2026

metrics.narrative: The business problem is modernising Source-to-Pay across the ANZ region ahead of a SAP ECC6 to S/4HANA migration, to get spend under management, enforce guided buying and PO-against-contract compliance. Tej Ram quantified the scale on the 12 Jan 2026 RFP briefing: roughly 110 million per annum flowing through POs and about 130 million per annum through a non-PO invoice process, with 10 power users and about 200 business users across Australia and New Zealand. No ROI, savings percentage, or cycle-time target has been articulated by the buyer, so the value case is sized by spend volume but not yet quantified as benefit. Quantifying the savings/automation impact with TTI's finance lead is the move.

sources: Salesforce Contact Role (Tej Ram, Evaluator, Primary)
champion.narrative: Tej Ram (Manager - Procurement & Transformation), the primary Salesforce contact and Evaluator, is an active operational champion: he project-manages the procurement side, keeps Zycus informed of timeline shifts, responded directly to Carl Kimball on 29-30 Jun, and has offered to set up a meeting to review the updated commercials (1 Jul 2026). His rating is developing rather than strong because he has not secured direct CFO engagement for Zycus, has not introduced Zycus to the US parent stakeholders who approve global costings, and the buyer flagged that Zycus fell short on the RACI/project plan competitors provided, a gap a strong champion would have pre-empted. Converting his advocacy into an executive introduction after the 13 Jul CFO meeting is the missing step.

competition.narrative: The field was named early and narrowed on evidence: the Jan 2026 RFP went to 3-4 vendors and the buyer referenced Coupa, iValua and Ariba (Next Step 13 Jan 2026); by 11 May 2026 TTI down-selected to two, naming GEP and iValua as the 'strong threats'. GEP is the single live current threat as a co-finalist with no disqualifying evidence. Coupa has weakened, with the buyer stating the S2C scope shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa' (1 Jul 2026), and Ariba has been silent since January. The wedge is the full S2P suite built on one codebase with Merlin AI differentiation (Merlin Intake in MS Teams, AP OCR/Smartdesk, agentic tail-spend negotiation), which Zycus should convert into a de-risked phased S2C plan and RACI sharper than GEP's to hold the down-select lead.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed operational pain is a legacy SAP ECC6 landscape being migrated to S/4HANA, which creates integration risk and a 'moving target' the buyer flagged directly, plus a need to bring spend under management, enforce PO-against-contract compliance and guided buying, and modernise tail-spend and invoicing (currently on SCM/escrow OCR). Tej Ram owns the procurement-transformation pain; Duncan Tennyson owns the ECC6/S/4HANA integration and change-management risk ('want proper and revised RACI, project plan', 1 Jul 2026). Inferred pain: adoption/change-management difficulty for ~200 business users, which is why Merlin Intake in MS Teams was positioned. The pain is real and board-sanctioned (a business case was built for CFO/board approval).

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partially visible: the RFP required vendors to upload a contract template and flag clauses in conflict with TTI's legal and contractual requirements (RFP section 4), and Duncan sent T&C documents from the Gartner BuySmart tool for Zycus legal review (email 19 May 2026, Adam to have legal review within EOW). Procurement runs through the Gartner BuySmart platform, with pricing and commercials handled off-platform by email. IT is gathering security requirements (InfoSec) per the 12 Dec 2025 catch-up. No MSA/SOW redline stage, signature authority, or contract-expiration forcing function has been reached yet; the S/4HANA migration is the timing dependency. A mutual close plan naming legal, InfoSec, and the parent-approval steps would create visibility.

sources: Avoma attendee lists (no CFO present on any call)
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is the Melbourne-based CFO, who is confirmed budget authority: he instructed the RFP be initiated ('CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January', Next Step 12 Dec 2025) and coordinates final sign-off with the TTI US parent company on costings. He has never appeared on a single Zycus-recorded call or commercial discussion across 16 months, and TTI is meeting him internally the week of 13 Jul 2026 to decide scope. His name is not disclosed in Salesforce, so he is mapped by role and authority but not engaged. The move is to have Tej secure a direct Zycus-to-CFO conversation immediately after the 13 Jul scope decision to validate budget and pricing.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a CFO-mandated, Gartner BuySmart-run RFP that TTI drove in structured stages readable from the call and activity sequence: written questionnaire responses (Feb) to onsite 3-hour demo (10 Mar) to down-select to two vendors (11 May) to reference checks with Nissan and TataPlay (completed 22-30 Jun) to hands-on sandbox and implementation deep-dive, then a CFO scope decision the week of 13 Jul, followed by global approval with the US parent before contract. Duncan Tennyson leads the IT/project side and Tej Ram leads procurement transformation; the CFO owns the award and coordinates parent-company sign-off on costings. The approval chain is local recommendation plus global (US parent) cost approval.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit from the RFP briefing and Next Step history: full S2P suite functional/technical fit against the Gartner BuySmart questionnaires (350 questions), integration with the current SAP ECC6 environment plus a credible path through the S/4HANA migration, quality of the implementation program and resourcing recommendations, customer references (Australian sites preferred), and commercial terms via a BAFO. Duncan Tennyson stated on 12 Jan 2026 that assessment weighs 'completeness of the submission... technical and functional responses... references... the implementation program'. A phased RACI and project plan has since become a hard criterion the buyer says competitors met and Zycus did not (Next Step 1 Jul 2026).

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 70.
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Tej Ram is an active operational champion and project lead who drives the RFP and keeps Zycus informed (responded to Carl 29-30 Jun, offered a review meeting 1 Jul), but strength is developing because he has not secured direct CFO engagement for Zycus, has not introduced the US-parent approvers, and did not pre-empt the RACI/project-plan gap the buyer flagged. The move is to have him arrange a direct CFO conversation right after the 13 Jul scope decision.

cro_panel.header: Techtronic Industries Company Limited — Shortlisted · $220K · closes 30 Jul

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 70 is carried by momentum 68 vs stage-expected 56; preference strength +1.00 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): Next Step carries 6 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 53 is anchored on the stage win (70), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

The 2 Jul 2025 demo showed the Merlin Intake / agentic negotiation differentiation, but the buyer has not engaged on it; a hands-on sandbox is the chance to surface AI-specific interest.

Last real buyer touch was an incoming email on 1 Jul 2026.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 20 Jan 2025, 894 days old is wrong; corrected: opp is 529 days old (Jan 2025 to Jul 2026). Stage Shortlisted since at least 11 May 2026 (53+ days in stage). Close date 30 Jul 2026 is 27 days out. Buyer's stated timeline (12 Jan 2026 call + Next Step): vendor selection targeted March 2026 (now 3+ months overdue), business-case approval a couple of months, contract Apr/May, solution-design kickoff deferred to July 2026 pending S/4HANA clarity, go-live H2. Close date already pulled in twice (23 Jun -> 30 Jun -> 30 Aug -> 30 Jul). Remaining path (13 Jul CFO scope decision -> revised pricing -> business case -> contract -> design kickoff) cannot complete by 30 Jul; forward slip of 60-90 days required.
evidence: Next Step 1 Jul 2026: 'likely to now move from S2P to S2C (P2P will come early new year). Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa. Now they want S2C revised pricing and implementation estimates/timelines. They are meeting CFO week of 13th July to decide on scope. This is all coming from the risk they feel moving from ECC to S4Hana, want proper and revised RACI, project plan, like all the other vendors gave them (we didn't).'
Next Step 30 Jun 2026: 'Tej Ram responded to Carl, they have completed references and are going through final stages of process.'
Task 22 Jun 2026 (email from Duncan/Tej): 'We have completed both the Nissan and Tata Play reference calls. The team will now review their notes.'

Next Step 11 May 2026: 'confirmed by email, shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox, need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here.'
Field history: CloseDate 2026-08-30 -> 2026-07-30 on 30 Jun 2026 (by Carl Kimball); earlier 2026-06-23 -> 2026-06-30 -> 2026-08-30.

RFP briefing call 12 Jan 2026 (Duncan Tennyson): 'RFP outcome by the March, we're looking to have made a decision on preferred supplier by then... we will need to put a pause in business case... we're really probably not looking at starting any sort of solution design work moving into implementation until July.'
Engagement pulse: last verified Salesforce activity 1 Jul 2026 (buyer email received, genuine two-way touch). Deal is LIVE.
north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is down-selected to the final two, has passed the reference-check phase (Nissan and TataPlay completed 22-30 Jun), and TTI has asked (1 Jul) for revised S2C-only pricing and phased implementation costing, but the 30 Jul close cannot absorb the CFO-led scope decision due the week of 13 Jul, business-case approval, and contract execution. The deal is healthy and engaged; the date will slip 60-90 days to the buyer's own H2 design kickoff.

flags: Convert Tej's operational advocacy into CFO sponsorship: secure a Zycus voice in or immediately after the 13 Jul scope decision, since the economic buyer has never met Zycus.
Close the deliverable gap GEP exploited: deliver the phased RACI and project plan TTI said only Zycus failed to provide, before the CFO scope meeting.
De-risk the ECC6 to S/4HANA integration with a concrete phased plan and Nissan/TataPlay reference proof to hold the down-select lead over GEP.
best_practice_check.summary: Zycus leads a down-select-to-two with references passed, but three levers decide the win: reaching the CFO, closing the RACI deliverable gap GEP exploited, and de-risking the S/4HANA integration story.

customer_preference.evidence: 1 Jul 2026: buyer requested Zycus-specific revised S2C pricing and phased implementation costing, and stated the S2C scope shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa' after down-selecting to two.

competitive_position.summary: GEP is the single strongest current threat as of Jul 2026: named alongside Zycus as a shortlist-to-two finalist and characterised as a 'strong threat' on 11 May 2026, with no evidence of elimination since. iValua was the other named 'strong threat' in May but has not recurred and has faded. Coupa has weakened, with the buyer stating on 1 Jul 2026 that the S2C scope shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa'. SAP Ariba was in the initial Jan 2026 field but silent since, effectively dormant. Recency-weighted rank: GEP (high, active finalist) > Coupa (low, faded/negative) > iValua (low, faded) > Ariba (dormant).
